rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO K. ARTUS VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, February 14, 2022, line 1. PETITIONER KAZUKO ARTUS' Petition For Writ Of Mandate. continued to February 22, 2022. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RALPH CAREY II VS. CATHERINE BAILEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, February 14, 2022, line 3. DEFENDANT CATHERINE BAILEY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is OVERRULED as to the cause of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment. This cause of action is sufficiently stated when all paragraphs incorporated by references are taken into consideration. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of written contract is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege the express contractual term breached. To the extent, as suggested in opposition, Plaintiff is alleging a breach of implied term, Plaintiff shall identify term(s) at issue on this cause of action that is not duplicative of the covenant of quiet enjoyment separately alleged in the complaint. Demurrer to the causes of action for misrepresentation is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting a causal link between the BMR-related misrepresentation/violation and damages/injury in fact/loss of money or property. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PETER HSIEH VS. LING C. LO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,500.00
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, February 14, 2022, line 4. DEFENDANT LING LO, WENDY LO's Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,500.00 DENIED. Sanctions granted in the amount of $7,887.50 jointly and severally against Los and their counsel, Lauren Jones, to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order.
